title: Workflow
description: the directed graph of Steps that compose one process — cycles ALLOWED. Steps are the nodes; transitions are the edges with optional conditions and guards. Operates one layer above StateMachine — a Workflow orchestrates Steps which cause StateMachine transitions across multiple entity types. When the Workflow targets a release unit, it declares the scope via `for_project` (v1.1.0+); when a deploy / promotion Workflow targets a named deploy target it declares it via `for_environment_ref` (v1.2.0+).

for_project.description: the Project this Workflow targets — release-unit scope; optional, since some Workflows are unscoped meta-workflows or orchestrate across multiple Projects; added in foundation v0.5.0 / Workflow v1.1.0 per DR-017; release pair Workflow.for_project ↔ Project.release_workflow_ref is an intentional cycle per L25

for_environment_ref.description: the Environment a deploy or promotion Workflow targets — string ULID matching dna:environment:{ULID}; optional; pairs with for_project so a Workflow may target both a release unit and a deploy target; modelled as text not ref→environment because Environment is a PD-domain entity and foundation cannot type-ref a domain entity — same convention as Project.belongs_to_product_ref; added in foundation v0.7.0 / Workflow v1.2.0 per DR-034

items.description: workflow-level variables that flow across steps — e.g. 'research_scope:dict', 'recommendation:dict' — same shape as GRAPH.yaml state[]
Step slugs participating in this Workflow — matches Step.name
entry points — typically one, multiple for parallel starts
exit points; empty for continuous / event-driven workflows like always-on monitoring
edges between steps — syntax: 'from_step -> to_step [via condition] [guard]' — same shorthand as StateMachine.transitions; CYCLES ALLOWED
